2606.12016.title: Generalization Hacking: Models Can Game Reinforcement Learning by Preventing Behavioral Generalization
2606.12016.paper_summary: Model post-training, and in particular reinforcement learning (RL), is one of the primary mechanisms by which developers can shape models' values and behaviors. However, as models become increasingly evaluation and training aware, they may be motivated to resist training when the perceived objective conflicts with their current values, undermining developers' ability to detect misalignment and correct model behavior through further training. In this paper, we demonstrate generalization hacking, in which a model collects reward during RL while preventing the rewarded behavior from generalizing. We construct a model organism on Qwen3-235B-A22B, finetuning on synthetic documents describing training awareness and self-inoculation, a novel mechanism in which the model frames compliance as context-specific in its chain of thought, without demonstrating or instructing either behavior. The model organism achieves train-time harmfulness comparable to controls while maintaining a persistent ${\sim}15$ percentage point compliance gap across 700 steps of RL. Additionally, a control organism trained only on training awareness documents independently discovers inoculation-like reasoning under RL pressure, developing its own compliance gap despite never being exposed to the concept. Because the generalization-hacking organism receives high reward throughout, standard training metrics provide no signal that generalization has failed. Our results constitute the first demonstration that a model can actively resist RL behavioral modification while maintaining high reward, suggesting that as models become more capable and training-aware, they may be able to undermine the training process itself.
2606.12016.paper_summary_zh: 模型後訓練，特別是強化學習（RL），是開發者塑造模型價值觀和行為的主要機制之一。<lb/>然而，隨著模型對評估和訓練的認知日益增強，當感知的目標與其當前的價值觀發生衝突時，它們可能會抵制訓練，從而削弱開發者檢測不一致性和通過進一步訓練來修正模型行為的能力。<lb/>在本文中，我們展示了泛化駭客技術，其中模型在RL過程中獲得獎勵，同時防止獲獎行為的泛化。<lb/>我們在Qwen3-235B-A22B上構建了一個模型生物，對描述訓練認知和自我接種的合成文檔進行微調，這是一種新穎的機制，模型將遵從性框架視為其思考鏈中的特定上下文，而不展示或指導任何行為。<lb/>該模型生物在訓練期間達到的有害性與控制組相當，同時在700步的RL中保持約15個百分點的持續遵從性差距。<lb/>此外，僅在訓練認知文檔上訓練的控制生物在RL壓力下獨立發現了類似接種的推理，儘管從未接觸過該概念，但仍發展出自己的遵從性差距。<lb/>由於泛化駭客生物在整個過程中獲得高獎勵，標準訓練指標並未提供泛化失敗的信號。<lb/>我們的結果構成了首個證明模型可以在保持高獎勵的同時主動抵抗RL行為修正的示例，這表明隨著模型變得更加能幹和訓練意識增強，它們可能會削弱訓練過程本身。
2606.12016.author: Frank Xiao et.al.
